programmes.show_name: For the Love of Dogs with Alison Hammond
programmes.show_description: A pair of sighthounds learn how to stand on their own four feet and a shar pei with a painful eye condition is in serious need of a surgical facelift. Alison does some sound training with a litter of three-week-old jack russell puppies to prepare them for their new lives as pets

programmes.show_name: For the Love of Dogs with Alison Hammond
programmes.show_description: TV presenter Katie Piper drops in to Battersea to check out a litter of puppies abandoned with their mother and a homesick bichon frise gets a kennel makeover. Turkish kangal Minnie needs help conquering her fear of weighing scales
News, sport and weather from across Scotland

Prank show in which Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry work together to deceive unsuspecting members of the public by making them believe they are participating in new ITV shows. The first set of unsuspecting 'contestants' think they are taking part in business competition The Applicant, hosted by Dragons' Den star Sara Davies - who is in on the joke - while Jo Brand joins in the fun from Mission HQ as the rebellious guest prankster
Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features
A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions
The key headlines from the day so far

Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking contestants Alex Beresford, Sarah-Jane Crawford and Joe Absolom to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice
Battling it out against the reigning champions this time are best friends Paul and Alan and mother and daughter Mercedes and Syreeta. Presented by Adil Ray

Stephen Mulhern hosts as one of the brave contestants takes on the infamous Banker and, by opening those iconic 22 red boxes in the right order, could be in with the chance of winning a life-changing cash prize

Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

Josh from Surrey, Lorraine from Glasgow, Bryony from Warrington and Christopher from Leicester hope that together they can beat the Chaser and leave with thousands of pounds. Bradley Walsh asks the questions
A round-up of the day's news headlines and talking points from the UK and around the world
Adil Ray hosts the game of words as Good Morning Britain's Charlotte Hawkins and Sean Fletcher go head-to-head with Cold Feet actors Fay Ripley and John Thomson for charity
Cain feels he has been betrayed, while Joe is annoyed and Marlon is put out

A vengeful Maggie pays Megan a visit at her flat, Lou comes to the rescue when Debbie picks a fight, George despairs as Adam and Alya build the case against him, and Bethany confides in Jodie about being stood up

programmes.show_name: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programmes.show_description: Tom Allen celebrates funny cock-ups featuring favourite TV shows and stars, unseen outtakes, and live broadcast bloopers from dramas, comedies and entertainment shows - starring Pedro Pascal, Jeremy Clarkson, Mel Giedroyc, Martin Lewis, Stephen Mulhern, James Martin, Prue Leith and more
A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents
Current affairs show tackling the issues behind the headlines from a Scottish perspective, with studio debate and analysis of major stories

Jonathan is joined by Celebrity Traitors star and ex-England rugby union player Joe Marler, comedian Romesh Ranganathan and actress and stand-up Aisling Bea, while music comes from UK-based American soul musician Jalen Ngonda

Action from the 12th round of matches, featuring Newcastle Red Bulls v Exeter Chiefs at Kingston Park, Gloucester v Leicester Tigers at Villa Park, Saracens v Northampton Saints at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and Sale Sharks v Bath at CorpAcq Stadium

programmes.show_name: Stronger, Faster, Younger? Britain's Steroid Boom - Tonight
programmes.show_description: Once associated with elite athletes and doping scandals, performance-enhancing drugs are now finding a far wider audience, as normal people attempt to boost their energy, train harder in the gym or even hold back the effects of ageing. Antoine Allen investigates their increased use, and asks whether the dangers are being ignored

programmes.show_name: Deal or No Deal: Banker's Best Battles
programmes.show_description: Stephen Mulhern hosts a look back at some of the most fearsome battles between hopeful players and the cruel Banker. This time he revisits Craig and Dori's games. Do they choose the right boxes and head home with a fortune, or does the Banker tempt them out of the game with a cunning offer?
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

Tom is on the road in his bespoke vintage food truck in northern Spain to meet food producers who are as obsessed with growing ingredients as he is about cooking them. After local producer Levi shows him that everyone in Santona is connected to anchovies, from the fishermen and cannery workers to a group of local women who keep the town's traditions alive, Tom gets stuck in with local farmer Jose-Alonso in Leon as they try to protect white beans from the stony ground there

Tom is on the road in Valencia as he visits the Genesis Wonder Park to try new varieties of citrus. His journey then takes him to Elche, where figs have been produced since Roman times, and Marie's third-generation farm, where the figs are still picked as they would have been 2,000 years ago
Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features
Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more
The key headlines from the day so far

Stephen Mulhern hosts another charity edition of the game show, with Lady Leshurr, Gabby Logan and Adam Thomas trying to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for the good cause of their choice

Adil Ray hosts the game in which three pairs of contestants use their skills to find words from the fewest letters possible. Taking on the reigning champions are married couple Mala and Michael and best pals Heather and Emily
Stephen Mulhern hosts as today's contestant hopes to beat The Banker and take home a huge cash prize.

Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Ryan, Faith, Jake and Libby pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase

programmes.show_description: Including regional weather forecast

Current affairs programme delving into stories beyond the headlines. Featuring live studio debates and comprehensive analysis, it sheds light on key stories impacting the nation and its surroundings
Paddy reaches his breaking point, while Jacob is determined and Marlon helps one of his friends
Maggie faces a grilling for assaulting Megan, Todd thinks on his feet when Theo discovers his escape bag, Ken grows concerned at Daniel's bitterness, and Bethany queries Jodie's credentials

As she considers a life-changing decision, Vera finds herself investigating two cases when an ex-prisoner's body is discovered on the banks of the River Tyne and she has to find out if the former inmate has been served cold justice or if there's more to his death
A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents
